finding: highlightChunk's codeToHtml.then has no per-request cancel guard; an in-flight highlight from a previous file/theme resolves after reset and writes stale HTML into the new highlighted map at index i
On a theme flip an in-flight old-theme codeToHtml can resolve after the reset and land in state, painting previous-theme colors and latching shikiBg to the old theme

observer effect deps [chunks,highlightChunk] don't change on theme flip, so after the ready-effect clears highlighted the observer is not recreated and won't re-fire for already-visible chunks, leaving them stuck plain

with OverlayScrollbars defer, osInstance() is null when the observer effect first runs; deps don't change on scroll so it never re-runs and the observer never attaches for the first file, so chunks beyond the eager 0/1 never highlight

on a transient codeToHtml failure the catch deletes the index from requestedRef but nothing re-drives an in-view chunk until it re-intersects; plain text is still readable (acceptable degradation, mitigated by observer re-attach)
setHighlighted(new Map(prev)) re-renders RawCodeView and re-evaluates all N chunk vdom divs on each highlight, O(chunks^2) reconciliation over a full scroll of a many-chunk file

plainHtml builds escaped HTML for every line on mount (O(N)); this is the intentional DEC-3 floor (all lines must be in the DOM for find-in-document), and the deferred-Shiki-tokenization is the real win, not the cheap plain-text build
buildPlainChunkHtml emits <pre tabindex=0> per chunk (shiki also stamps tabindex on its pre), turning one code tab-stop into one per chunk; a keyboard user must tab through every chunk

find-active scroll: each chunk plain->highlight swap is a subtree mutation firing the debounced O(N) TreeWalk rebuild; inherent to windowing + not modified per ITEM-3, and the rAF debounce coalesces bursts to ~one rebuild/frame

chunkReservedHeight uses a flat 44px/line wrap estimate so mixed-length wrapped chunks mis-reserve, but contain-intrinsic-size:auto remembers the measured size after first paint so jitter is pre-first-paint only
comment says the observer 'Mirrors pdf/body.tsx exactly' but rootMargin is 600px (pdf 200px) and prefetch is idx+1 only (pdf idx+1..+3); doc overstatement, not a behavior bug
the CSV 'truncation fires above cap' test asserts n>CONST arithmetic that never calls parseDelimitedText, re-implementing the predicate instead of exercising the real truncated:true slice branch

the XLSX truncation test defines its own truncatedAt=n>XLSX_MAX_ROWS predicate; the real predicate lives inline in XlsxBody.tsx and parse.ts only exports the constant, so production xlsx truncation code is never exercised

asserting .line-code span[style*=color] to prove on-demand highlight risks flake given Shiki does not apply under the vite preview build; the file still renders plain if Shiki fails, timing out the poll

escapeHtml omits quotes but user content is interpolated ONLY as element text-content and line numbers only as computed integers (no attribute takes user data), so &<> escaping is complete for this injection context; no XSS
truncation banner + readout copy is byte-consistent across RawCodeView/DelimitedTable/XlsxBody; no wording drift
RawCodeView props (text/filename?/wordWrap?) unchanged; all callers (text/web/markdown/tabular body.tsx) pass the same shape; no contract break
no authorization path touched; seedProjectBinaryFile is a test fixture using the admin token against the existing upload endpoint (mirrors seedProjectImage)
